# MAEI Project File Structure

This document explains what every file in the `MAEI_Project` directory does and where to find your data. 

## 📁 `data/`
This folder contains the raw inputs and the processed outputs used to run the math.
*   **`raw/`**: Contains the untouched, original CSV files mapping O*NET codes to their descriptions, abilities, tasks, and the original 2013 Frey & Osborne probabilities.
*   **`processed/`**: Contains `modeling_dataset.pkl` which is the giant compiled matrix generated by our very first script containing all the raw 1016 occupations with their NLP topics converted into math.

## 📁 `src/`
This folder contains the actual Python scripts that process the data from start to finish. You must run them in order.
*   **`01_data_preparation.py`**: Merges the original F&O baseline against modern O*NET variables, extracts task vocabulary into TF-IDF frequencies, and compiles everything into a massive machine learning matrix. 
*   **`02_baseline_modeling.py`**: Condenses the NLP TF-IDF frequencies into 10 explicit, mathematically balanced topics (e.g. Sales, Systems, Education) mapping true computational word associations. It trains robust machine learning baseline models (Random Forest, XGBoost) using 2013 automation definitions as the baseline reference point. 
*   **`03_maei_calculation.py`**: The mathematical core. It applies custom multipliers generated from authoritative 2026 AI papers (Eloundou, Noy & Zhang) targeting features LLMs excel at (like programming and data processing). It also applies a broad 'Uplift' capturing the pervasiveness of generative AI tools before writing out the final `results`. 
*   **`04_validation_and_reporting.py`**: Compares the 2013 output structurally to the 2026 theoretical outcome across massive ranges of variables (e.g., heavily weighting coding and slightly weighting text). It mathematically confirms model outputs are numerically stable with consistent relative rankings. It also automatically generates the actual graphs located in the `figures/` folder showing the "Paradigm Shift" in cognitive data overlap.
*   **`05_external_validation.py`**: A defensive script that downloads the completely independent AIOE AI-exposure dataset created by Felten, Raj, and Seamans in 2021. It runs a Spearman rank correlation metric against our 1016 SOCs to confirm that our custom MAEI math actually matches external academic reality. 

## 📁 `results/`
Contains the final output spreadsheets that answer "What did the model find?"
*   **`maei_2026_with_deltas.csv`**: The master spreadsheet containing all 1016 SOC roles with their History 2013 anchors vs the brand-new 2026 calculated AI overlap score and the explicit mathematical delta.
*   **`maei_explanations.csv`**: Breaks down the actual logic behind individual scores, citing which O*NET features drove the score upward and giving specific human explanations as to why. 
*   **`feature_importance.csv`**: Ranks exactly which O*NET features drove the fundamental baseline machine learning predictions.
*   **`sensitivity_analysis.csv`**: The raw numerical outcomes from the `04_validation` stress testing, showing how changing the W (Exposure) and P (Protection) variables barely alters the core rankings. 

## 📁 `figures/`
Contains all the graphs and images explaining the model outputs.
*   **`ablation_pure_vs_anchored.png`**: Separates our 2026 custom math away from the historical 2013 anchor logic and charts exactly what the delta disparity looks like.
*   **`exposure_heatmap.png`**: Groups huge families of jobs (like Management or Law) together and uses color (Blue vs Red) to show which categories have highest overlap with AI in 2026.
*   **`scatter_hist_vs_maei.png`**: Plots occupations against a perfect diagonal line — if it sits above the line, the job’s exposure fundamentally rose between 2013 and 2026. 
*   **`external_validation_aioe.png`**: The plot generated by script `05` displaying the MAEI mapping structurally against the AIOE matrix and finding a firm inverse-correlation mathematically proving structural inversion. 

## 📁 `docs/`
Contains the theory and project narrative.
*   **`METHODOLOGY.md`**: The master theory document explaining the "why" heavily over the "how." It details the AI capability factors used, defends the historical baselines and defines explicitly why the 1-100 score implies "Feature Overlap Tracking" rather than "Destruction Probability." It's written plainly to be easily readable to a professor or stakeholder defending the project's logic.
